objective: Phase 2 of the CODEREF-CORE pipeline refactor. Split relationship extraction into typed raw-fact records (import, call, export, header-import placeholder) without resolution. Raw facts must carry every detail downstream resolvers need: aliases, dynamic flag, type-only flag, receiver text, scope path, callee identity context. No raw fact may claim to be a graph edge.

in: RawImportFact type with fields: sourceElementId, sourceFile, moduleSpecifier, specifiers (with alias bindings), defaultImport, namespaceImport, typeOnly, dynamic, line
RawCallFact type preserving receiverText, scopePath, callExpressionText (full source slice), calleeName, line, language, sourceElementCandidate
RawExportFact type with sourceFile, exportedName, localName, kind (named|default|reexport|namespace), line
RawHeaderImportFact PLACEHOLDER type with sourceFile, rawString (the literal '<module>:<symbol>' from @imports header), parseStatus='placeholder' (no actual parsing — Phase 2.5)
Refactor of src/pipeline/extractors/relationship-extractor.ts to emit the four typed arrays via PipelineState
Update orchestrator + projection seam to thread raw facts (additive on projections.json)
Five new test suites: raw-import-facts, raw-call-facts, raw-export-facts, raw-header-import-placeholders, no-graph-edge-claim (the invariant enforcer)
out: Graph construction or graph node ID writes on relationship records (Phase 5)
Making the Phase 0 ground-truth tests pass — those become green in Phase 3/4, NOT Phase 2

hard_constraints: Phase 2 hard-stops before Phase 2.5 / Phase 3 / Phase 4. Do not begin resolver implementation in this workorder.
No raw fact may carry a field that names a graph node ID as endpoint. Enforced by no-graph-edge-claim.test.ts.
Method calls MUST preserve receiver text. obj.save() is RawCallFact{ receiverText='obj', calleeName='save' }, never bare 'save'.
Scope path MUST be populated for every call. Walk enclosing functions/classes during AST traversal.
Header-import facts in this phase are PLACEHOLDERS only. No BNF parsing. No @imports validation. No layer/capability/constraint extraction.
Phase 0 ground-truth tests MUST CONTINUE TO FAIL for their original reasons. If a ground-truth test starts passing during this WO, that is a regression in Phase 2's discipline (resolution leaked in).
populate-coderef CLI surface unchanged (flag names, defaults, exit behavior).
ground_truth_test_disposition: Per roadmap and Gemini prep analysis: the 6 Phase 0 ground-truth tests do NOT pass in Phase 2. Phase 2 produces facts; Phase 3 resolves imports; Phase 4 resolves calls. Tests start passing incrementally across Phase 3/4. The original prep brief stating 'Phase 2 is the phase where the 6 tests start passing' was incorrect — Gemini caught this and adjudication confirmed Gemini's reading against roadmap line 143-184.
